Title: A rice fungal MAMP-responsive MAPK cascade regulates metabolic flow to antimicrobial metabolite synthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimicrobial defense metabolites in rice innate immunity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator for chitin elicitor-induced diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis; required for full accumulation of momilactones and phytocassanes and for OsMKK4DD-induced cell death
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation associated with cell death and suppression of basic cellular activities via the cascade
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: osmpk6 knockout mutant analysis; complementation with osmpk6/OsMPK6; in-gel kinase assay; immunoprecipitation kinase assay; chitin elicitor treatment; HPLC-ESI-MS/MS phytoalexin quantification; microarray comparison in osmpk6 versus osmpk6/OsMPK6 backgrounds
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: chitin elicitor (fungal MAMP); dexamethasone-inducible OsMKK4DD expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chitin elicitor activates OsMPK6 in rice cells. 2. osmpk6 mutants show strongly reduced accumulation of momilactones and phytocassanes after elicitation, and complementation restores the phenotype. 3. OsMPK6 is required for OsMKK4DD-induced up-regulation of DXP/diterpenoid phytoalexin pathway genes, but not for most phenylpropanoid pathway induction. 4. OsMPK6 is essential for OsMKK4DD-induced cell death.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsMPK6 is the decisive downstream MAPK for metabolic redirection to diterpenoid phytoalexins in this study; OsMPK3 may act redundantly for some non-diterpenoid responses but was not shown as the main determinant of phytoalexin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated rapidly and transiently after chitin elicitor treatment in cultured rice cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: cultured cells after chitin elicitor treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling target to enhance inducible antimicrobial diterpenoid phytoalexin production, but deployment must balance defense activation against cell death and growth suppression
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsMPK6 upstream promoter in complementation line; dexamethasone-inducible promoter used for upstream OsMKK4DD activation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimicrobial defense metabolites and defense-associated cell wall reinforcement
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rice suspension cell culture medium for momilactones/phytocassanes; rice cell wall fraction for lignin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of metabolic flow toward diterpenoid phytoalexins and lignin after MAMP signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsMPK3 and OsMPK6; induces DXP pathway and diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthetic genes including CPS2, CPS4, KSL4, KSL7 and KSL8; also induces phenylpropanoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: chitin elicitor activates OsMKK4; dexamethasone-inducible expression of constitutively active OsMKK4DD
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsMKK4DD did not induce extracellular ROS in rice despite inducing cell death, but did induce ROS/cell death in Nicotiana benthamiana; regulation of diterpenoid pathway is largely OsMPK6-dependent whereas phenylpropanoid induction is largely OsMPK6-independent
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsMPK3; OsMPK6; DXP pathway genes; CPS2; CPS4; KSL4; KSL7; KSL8; phenylpropanoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defense-associated metabolic reprogramming; diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis; phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis; metabolic flow from glycolysis to secondary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Induces extensive cell death, seedling lethality/growth arrest, and repression of translation/cell-cycle genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major upstream switch that reprograms metabolism toward antimicrobial diterpenoid phytoalexin synthesis upon fungal MAMP signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chitin elicitor activates OsMKK4. 2. Constitutively active OsMKK4DD activates OsMPK3 and OsMPK6 in vivo. 3. OsMKK4DD induces broad transcriptome reprogramming favoring glycolysis, DXP pathway, diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis and phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis. 4. OsMKK4DD causes accumulation of momilactones, phytocassanes and lignin. 5. Diterpenoid pathway induction is mainly OsMPK6-dependent, whereas phenylpropanoid pathway induction is largely OsMPK6-independent.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MAPK cascade with downstream OsMPK3 and OsMPK6
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro kinase assay; HA-tag IP kinase assay; dexamethasone-inducible expression of OsMKK4DD/OsMKK4WT; microarray; HPLC-ESI-MS/MS phytoalexin measurement; lignin staining and quantification; genetic analysis in osmpk6 and osmpk6/OsMPK6 backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Kinase activity increased within 5 min after chitin elicitor treatment in cultured rice cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein kinase-mediated activation of downstream MAPKs and transcriptional reprogramming of metabolic genes

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rice suspension cell culture medium for phytoalexins; cell wall fraction for lignin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated by chitin elicitor and by OsMKK4DD; implicated as a potentially redundant regulator with OsMPK6, but not directly demonstrated as essential for phytoalexin accumulation in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core cascade component because it is a principal experimental subject in results; however, its causal role in nutrient-metabolite accumulation is weaker than OsMPK6 and not genetically resolved here.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defense MAPK signaling potentially contributing to metabolic reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible lethality when combined with osmpk6 loss, suggesting essential redundant function
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established as decisive alone; suggested redundant component of the OsMKK4-OsMPK3/OsMPK6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chitin elicitor activates OsMPK3. 2. OsMKK4 activates OsMPK3 in vitro and in vivo. 3. OsMPK3 may redundantly mediate parts of OsMKK4-dependent responses that are less dependent on OsMPK6, such as phenylpropanoid-related responses, but this was not directly proven.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MAPK cascade with upstream OsMKK4
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: immunoprecipitation kinase assay; in vitro kinase assay with OsMKK4; DEX-inducible OsMKK4DD activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly activated by chitin elicitor in cultured rice cells; activated after OsMKK4DD induction
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: dexamethasone-inducible promoter used for upstream OsMKK4DD activation
